description: A daily geography guessing game: Hangman-style letter guessing against a countdown clock, while the target country's outline (and its neighbors') draw themselves in as hints.

userStories.title: Scaffold the Vite + React + TypeScript project
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a working project skeleton so subsequent stories have somewhere to build.
acceptanceCriteria: Project created via the Vite react-ts template
Framer Motion added as a dependency
Dev server starts and renders a placeholder page with npm run dev
Typecheck passes
Verify placeholder page loads in browser using automate-browser skill

userStories.title: Generate country outline + centroid data from world-atlas TopoJSON
userStories.description: As a developer, I need per-country SVG outline paths and centroid coordinates so the game can render and position any country.
acceptanceCriteria: Build-time script loads world-atlas TopoJSON at 50m resolution
Script converts each country's geometry to an SVG path string using an equal-area or equirectangular projection (not Mercator)
Script computes each country's centroid as { lat, lng }
Output written to src/data/countries-geo.json, keyed by ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 or alpha-3 code (pick one and use it consistently across all later stories)
Disputed territories are included/excluded/labeled exactly as world-atlas's Natural Earth source provides them, with no manual overrides
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Merge neighbor adjacency, unique-letter count, and island flag into country data
userStories.description: As a developer, I need each country's neighbor list and name-difficulty metadata so daily selection and gameplay logic have what they need.
acceptanceCriteria: Script fetches or uses a local snapshot of restcountries.com's `borders` field to attach neighbor_codes: string[] per country (same ISO code scheme as US-002)
Script computes unique_letters: number from each country's name (case-insensitive, letters only)
Script computes is_island: boolean as (neighbor_codes.length === 0)
Merged output written to src/data/countries.json combining this with US-002's outline/centroid data
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: restcountries.com is fully deprecated (confirmed by US-002); used the `world-countries` npm package as a local static snapshot of the same `borders` data instead. Special-cased Kosovo's code mismatch (world-countries uses 'UNK', US-002's geo data uses the slug 'KOSOVO' since it has no ISO numeric id) and dropped one documented restcountries data quirk (Sri Lanka listed as bordering India via the long-submerged Adam's Bridge) since CLAUDE.md explicitly names Sri Lanka as a 0-land-neighbor island example. Border references to non-sovereign territories absent from world-atlas's topology (Gibraltar, French Guiana, etc.) are dropped rather than pointing at countries with no outline data.

userStories.title: Implement deterministic daily-country and neighbor-subset selection
userStories.description: As a player, I want the same target country as every other player today, so the shareable result is comparable.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/game/dailyCountry.ts exports a function taking a UTC date and returning today's target country via a deterministic hash: hash(UTC date) mod country_count

The same hash-based approach selects up to 3 neighbors when a country has more than 3 (documented explicitly in code comments as the chosen tie-breaking rule, since the PRD leaves the exact selection algorithm unspecified beyond 'deterministic, seeded like the daily country')
Countries with 0 neighbors (is_island) return an empty neighbor list without error
Given the same date input, the function returns identical output on every call (unit test verifies this)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Ambiguity flagged: PRD does not specify HOW to pick which 3 neighbors when a country has more than 3 (e.g. largest shared border vs. alphabetical vs. hashed order). This story picks and documents the hashed-order approach for consistency with daily-country selection — revisit if a different rule is wanted later.

userStories.title: Build the target-country outline draw-on animation component
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see the target country's outline draw itself in, so the drawing acts as a visual hint and implicit timer.
acceptanceCriteria: A CountryOutline component accepts a country's SVG path and a completion percentage (0-100) prop
The component animates stroke reveal via Framer Motion's pathLength/pathSpacing props, not manual getTotalLength()
Rendering today's target country (from US-004) at various completion percentages visibly changes how much of the outline is drawn
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using automate-browser skill

userStories.title: Build the countdown clock and unique-letter-scaled penalty state machine
userStories.description: As a player, I want wrong guesses to cost me time proportional to the puzzle's real difficulty, so easy names aren't trivially free.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/game/clock.ts implements a 60-second countdown, ticking down in real time

A wrong-letter-guess event subtracts time per a configurable penalty-tier table (exported as a named constant, not hardcoded inline): illustrative starting values are <=5 unique letters -> 20s, 6-9 -> 15s, 10+ -> 10s, clearly commented as placeholder values pending playtesting
Clock reaching 0 transitions state to 'failed'
An explicit stop/give-up call also transitions state to 'failed' immediately
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Ambiguity flagged: exact penalty values are explicitly TBD in the PRD (needs playtesting) — implement as an easily-tunable config table, not as a design decision this story finalizes.

userStories.title: Build the Hangman letter-guess UI and wire it to the clock and outline
userStories.description: As a player, I want to guess letters on-screen or via my keyboard and see immediate feedback, so I can solve the puzzle.
acceptanceCriteria: An on-screen A-Z keyboard component is the visual source of truth, showing per-letter state: unguessed / correct / wrong
A global keydown listener for a-z dispatches to the same guess-submission handler as clicking an on-screen key
A correct guess fills every instance of that letter in the target name's blanks at once
A wrong guess triggers the penalty from US-006's clock and updates the on-screen key to the 'wrong' state
A visible 'give up' button ends the round as 'failed' immediately
Filling in the complete target name transitions round state to 'solved'
The target outline's draw completion (US-005) is driven by elapsed time on the clock (US-006), covering the first ~40-50% of the 60s budget
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using automate-browser skill

userStories.title: Render neighbor country outlines at compass-accurate positions with staggered onset
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see up to 3 neighboring countries appear in their real-world direction once I've used enough time, so I get a spatial hint layer.
acceptanceCriteria: Neighbor outlines (reusing the CountryOutline component from US-005) remain hidden while time remaining is above the ~40-50% staggered-onset threshold
Once time remaining drops below that threshold, each of today's up-to-3 neighbor countries (from US-004) begins drawing in
Each neighbor is positioned at the nearest of a fixed 8-point compass anchor (N, NE, E, SE, S, SW, W, NW) around the target, computed from the neighbor's centroid relative to the target's centroid
When two neighbors round to the same compass anchor, the second is offset to an adjacent unused anchor rather than overlapping the first
Countries with fewer than 3 neighbors (including 0, for islands) simply show fewer neighbor slots, with no error or placeholder box
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using automate-browser skill
userStories.notes: Ambiguity flagged: PRD specifies 'nearest anchor point' without naming a specific anchor count or collision rule. This story commits to an 8-point compass with adjacent-anchor offsetting on collision — reasonable default, but revisit if it looks wrong once real neighbor geometry is on screen. Suggested skills: automate-browser, build-ui

userStories.title: Reveal neighbor name labels letter-by-letter in randomized order
userStories.description: As a player, I want each visible neighbor's name to slowly spell itself out in a non-obvious order, so it functions as an escalating (not instant) hint.
acceptanceCriteria: Each neighbor slot shows a redacted name label (blanks) as soon as its outline begins appearing (per US-008)
Letters of each neighbor's name reveal automatically over time in a randomized (not left-to-right) order — this is not player-guessed input
The reveal order is randomized once per round per neighbor, not re-randomized on every re-render
By the time the clock reaches 0, each visible neighbor's full name is revealed
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using automate-browser skill

userStories.title: Display the trivia fact overlay on the target country's outline
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see an interesting fact about the target country while its outline draws, so the wait has some content beyond the animation.
acceptanceCriteria: A text overlay renders on top of the target CountryOutline, showing the target country's trivia_fact string
Component reads from src/data/trivia.json, keyed by the same ISO code as countries.json
If no fact exists yet for a country (expected for most countries until US-013 lands), the overlay is hidden rather than showing blank/undefined text
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using automate-browser skill
userStories.notes: Deliberately decoupled from trivia content generation (US-013) — ships against a stub/partial src/data/trivia.json so UI work isn't blocked on content authoring. Suggested skills: automate-browser

userStories.title: Persist streak state to localStorage
userStories.description: As a player, I want my consecutive-day streak remembered between visits, so the daily-ritual hook actually works.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/streak/index.ts reads/writes { current_streak, longest_streak, last_played_date } to localStorage
On round outcome 'solved', current_streak increments if last_played_date was yesterday (UTC), resets to 1 otherwise; longest_streak updates if exceeded
On round outcome 'failed' (timeout or give-up), current_streak resets to 0
Streak value is read on app load and reflects the persisted state without requiring a fresh guess
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Supabase is explicitly out of scope for this story and for v1 generally — localStorage only.

userStories.title: Generate and display the spoiler-safe shareable result string
userStories.description: As a player, I want to copy a compact result summary that doesn't spoil the answer for people who haven't solved it yet.
acceptanceCriteria: On round end, a share string is generated containing: a day number, the outcome (time remaining if solved, or 'failed'), and a colored guess-pattern row (correct vs. wrong letter guesses, no letters shown)
The target country's name/flag appears in the share string ONLY when the outcome is 'solved' — a failed round's share string omits the country identity entirely
A visible 'copy' button copies the exact share string to the clipboard
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using automate-browser skill
userStories.notes: Exact visual format (colors/emoji) is not fully specified in the PRD — this story should pick a concrete, readable format and document it in code comments as the working default. Suggested skills: automate-browser

userStories.title: Author LLM-generated, human-reviewed trivia facts for all countries
userStories.description: As a player, I want a genuinely interesting, verifiably true fact for whichever country comes up, so the trivia overlay is worth reading.
acceptanceCriteria: src/data/trivia.json contains one trivia_fact string per country present in src/data/countries.json (~190 entries)
Each fact is LLM-generated in a punchy, specific, surprising tone (matching the reference example: 'This country has enough nuclear fallout shelters to house more than 100% of its population')

Every generated fact is flagged in the story's notes as PENDING HUMAN REVIEW — this story does not mark itself fully 'passes: true' on generation alone; a human must review for factual accuracy before the data is considered final
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is a content-authoring task with a mandatory human-review gate, not a pure coding task — do not let an autonomous loop iteration silently treat LLM-generated trivia as fact-checked. Flag for explicit human review before shipping, per the locked PRD decision.
